title: Woodland Wins Houston Open
excerpt: Gary Woodland takes the top spot at the Texas Children's Houston Open, finishing -18 overall
body:
Texas Children's Houston Open Recap

Gary Woodland has taken the top spot at the Texas Children's Houston Open, finishing -18 overall and claiming the number one position on the leaderboard. Woodland, who played with Titleist, finished 1 stroke ahead of Nicolai Højgaard, who came in second at -17.

DP World Tour Update

Meanwhile, on the DP World Tour, Eugenio Chacarra is currently in the lead at the DLF G&CC in Gurugram, India, with a four-stroke lead going into Sunday. Chacarra will be looking to hold onto his lead and take home the win.

LIV Golf Latest

LIV Golf has seen some exciting action recently, with the 4Aces GC team taking the top spot at the Sentosa Golf Club in Singapore, finishing -27 overall. Ripper GC came in second, just behind the leaders.

Woodland's Winning Score
-18
Højgaard's Second Place Score
-17
Chacarra's Lead on DP World Tour
4 strokes

Golf News Roundup

Other notable golfers, such as Min Woo Lee and Michael Thorbjornsen, finished tied for third at -12, while Sam Stevens and Jason Day came in tied for fifth at -11. The Texas Children's Houston Open saw some impressive play, with many golfers putting in strong performances throughout the tournament.

The competition is heating up on the PGA Tour, with many talented golfers vying for the top spot. As the season continues, it will be exciting to see who comes out on top.
